name: Mali

outbound_official.note: no official Mali government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

visa_free.notes: ECOWAS free movement protocol (effective April 30, 1980) exempts nationals of Benin, Burkina Faso, Cape Verde, Côte d'Ivoire, Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone, and Togo from entry visas to Mali. Mali formally withdrew from ECOWAS on January 29, 2025, but a joint AES-ECOWAS declaration of December 14, 2024 confirmed that AES countries remain a visa-free zone for all ECOWAS nationals, and ECOWAS reciprocally extended the same rights to AES nationals until further notice. As of June 2026 no formal revocation has been announced. Re-confirmed by maliembassy.us in June 2026 validation run.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. 3-month maximum stay. Re-confirmed on maliembassy.us in June 2026 validation run.

Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. Listed on the official Mali Embassy in Washington DC visa page. Max stay not specified on any official source. Re-confirmed on maliembassy.us in June 2026 validation run.

Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. Listed on the official Mali Embassy in Washington DC visa page. Max stay not specified on any official source. Re-confirmed on maliembassy.us in June 2026 validation run.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. 3-month maximum stay. Re-confirmed on maliembassy.us in June 2026 validation run.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. 3-month maximum stay. Re-confirmed on maliembassy.us in June 2026 validation run.

Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. Listed on the official Mali Embassy in Washington DC visa page. Max stay not specified on any official source. Re-confirmed on maliembassy.us in June 2026 validation run.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. 3-month maximum stay. Re-confirmed on maliembassy.us in June 2026 validation run.
Bilateral visa exemption; valid identity card or passport acceptable. 3-month maximum stay. Re-confirmed on maliembassy.us in June 2026 validation run.

Visa-free for UAE regular (ordinary) passport holders per bilateral MoU signed November 1, 2018 between UAE and Mali. Gulf News confirmed UAE nationals with diplomatic, private (ordinary), and VIP passports are covered. Wikipedia also confirms UAE ordinary passport holders are visa-free (90 days). NOTE: maliembassy.us does not list UAE in its visa-exempt nationalities list as of June 2026 validation run; UAE is not among the named countries on that page. The MoU grant in the Gulf News article covers UAE nationals travelling TO Mali (the correct direction for this inbound record). The UAE MOFA page was separately checked and shows Malian citizens need a visa to enter UAE (the reverse direction). The UAE-Mali MoU is kept based on the 2018 bilateral agreement news; flagged for re-verification against official Malian MFA text.

Visa-free as of July 2025 per Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali). Max stay not specified. No independent official Mali government source confirmed; flagged as unverified from official Malian pages. Preserved per safety rule (cannot disprove from official source).

Visa-free (30 days) per Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali). No independent official Mali government source confirmed; flagged as unverified from official Malian pages. Preserved per safety rule (cannot disprove from official source).

visa_on_arrival.notes: Rwandan nationals may obtain a visa on arrival for a maximum 3-month stay per Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali). This replaced a former broader visa-on-arrival policy that ended March 9, 2015. No independent official Mali government page confirmed; flagged. Preserved per safety rule (cannot disprove from official source).

e_visa.notes: Visa applications and payments are processed fully online via the Malian Ministry of Foreign Affairs website. An entry permit is issued electronically once approved. There is no need to send documents to a Malian Embassy. Confirmed by the UK FCDO travel advice page (updated June 2026) which references the portal at diplomatiemmc.gouv.ml/vitrine/. The Mali Embassy in Washington DC also links to this portal for all visa applications. The MFA portal (diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml/vitrine/) was re-checked in June 2026 - it presents an interactive nationality checker ('Ai-je besoin d'un visa pour voyager au Mali?') with ordinary, diplomatic, special, refugee, and UN laissez-passer passport types. Note: the portal URL appearing in official sources varies slightly between 'diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml' and 'diplomatiemmc.gouv.ml'.

visa_policy.notes: Mali requires a visa for all nationalities except those listed as visa-free. Visa applications are processed online at diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml (portal also cited as diplomatiemmc.gouv.ml in some official sources). Diplomatic and official passport holders benefit from free 3-year multiple-entry visas (US Embassy Mali source). Yellow fever vaccination certificate is mandatory for entry. As of January 1, 2026, the Government of Mali imposed a reciprocal entry ban on U.S. citizens matching the conditions applied to Malian nationals by the US, following US Presidential Proclamation 10998 (December 16, 2025). Exceptions exist for US nationals who are permanent residents of Mali, hold a currently valid Mali visa, are athletes, diplomats, or whose entry serves the interests of the republic. Syria nationals are refused entry and transit. Mali formally withdrew from ECOWAS on January 29, 2025, and joined the Alliance of Sahel States (AES / Confédération des États du Sahel) with Burkina Faso and Niger; free movement for ECOWAS nationals continues per the AES-ECOWAS joint declaration of December 14, 2024.

visa_types.name: Mali Entry Visa (Ordinary Passport)
visa_types.purpose: General entry visa for tourism, business, and other purposes for foreign nationals holding ordinary passports who are not visa-exempt.
visa_types.notes: Fee of $185 USD applies to all visa types except diplomatic/official, per the Mali Embassy in Washington DC. Validity up to 5 years, multiple entry. Applications submitted online via the Ministry of Foreign Affairs portal at diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml/vitrine/ - no physical embassy submission required for most applicants. Visas are NOT issued on arrival at Malian airports. Yellow fever vaccination certificate mandatory. US citizens are currently subject to a reciprocal entry ban effective January 1, 2026 (with limited exceptions).

visa_types.name: Mali Entry Visa (Business - Ordinary Passport)
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for foreign nationals travelling to Mali for business activities, meetings, or commercial purposes.
visa_types.notes: Mali does not publish a separately named business visa category on official embassy pages. Business travel uses the same general entry visa as tourist travel. Fee $185 USD; up to 5-year validity, multiple entry confirmed by maliembassy.us. Online application via diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml/vitrine/.

visa_types.name: Mali Diplomatic / Official Visa
visa_types.purpose: Visa for holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports travelling to Mali on behalf of their government or an international organization.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge; 3-year validity, multiple entry per maliembassy.us. Covers diplomatic passport holders and holders of laissez-passer for international organizations. A note verbale from the sending government may be required (noted by ambamali.ca). Some nationalities holding diplomatic/service passports are exempt from visa requirements entirely.

visa_types.name: Mali Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry authorization for foreign nationals transiting through Mali en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Transit visa is referenced as a passport type/purpose category in the Mali Ministry of Foreign Affairs online portal (diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml/vitrine/), which accepts applications by nationality and passport type. Specific fee and duration not published on accessible official pages. Syrian nationals are refused even airside transit. Apply online via the MFA portal.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic and service category passports of Brazil, China, Cuba, Russia, and Rwanda do not require a visa for a maximum period of 90 days. Chinese nationals with public affairs passports also receive 90 days.

Any national holding a diplomatic or service passport receives up to one month visa-free access per Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali). Note: This is unconfirmed by directly-read official Malian pages (maliembassy.us, diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml). The Mali Embassy Canada page shows diplomatic passport holders must still present a 'note verbale', potentially contradicting a blanket exemption.

nationalities.notes: Entry and transit refused to Syrian nationals, even if not leaving the aircraft. Per Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali). Not verified from a directly-accessed official Malian government page.

As of January 1, 2026, Mali imposed a reciprocal entry and transit ban on US citizens matching the conditions imposed by the US on Malian citizens under US Presidential Proclamation 10998 (effective December 16, 2025, which placed Mali on a US travel ban list). Mali's Ministry of Foreign Affairs announced on January 2, 2026 that the same conditions and requirements applied by the US to Malian nationals would be applied to US nationals seeking entry into Mali. US State Department confirmed 'the government of Mali has suspended visas to U.S. citizens.' Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali, re-fetched June 2026) states: 'Entry and transit is refused to Syria and United States nationals, even if not leaving the aircraft and proceeding by the same flight.' Exceptions to the Mali ban include: US citizens who are permanent residents of Mali, holders of a currently valid Mali visa, athletes, diplomats, and persons whose entry serves the interests of the republic. Entry of US citizens into Mali remains at the sole discretion of the Government of Mali.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found for Mali on any official government source. No official government announcement or program portal was found establishing any CBI or investor-citizenship pathway.

agreements: ECOWAS free movement protocol (historical - Mali formally withdrew January 29, 2025; AES-ECOWAS joint declaration of December 14, 2024 confirmed ECOWAS nationals remain visa-free in AES states until further notice; as of June 2026 no formal revocation announced)

Alliance of Sahel States (AES) / Confédération des États du Sahel - free movement area among Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger; AES biometric ID card launched December 2025 (Burkina Faso); common AES visa under development

UAE-Mali bilateral visa exemption MoU (signed November 1, 2018) - covers UAE ordinary, diplomatic, VIP passports for entry into Mali; direction of exemption for Mali diplomatic passports in UAE to be confirmed

validation_notes: 2026-06-29 VALIDATION RUN: Re-fetched maliembassy.us/visas/ - confirmed visa-free list unchanged: ECOWAS member states, Algeria, Andorra, Cameroon, Chad, Gambia, Morocco, Mauritania, Monaco, Tunisia. NOTE: This run found Gambia listed explicitly by name on the page in addition to the ECOWAS bloc reference ('No visa is required for ECOWAS countries citizens, citizens from Algeria, Cameroon, Andorra, Monaco, Chad, Gambia, Morocco, Mauritania, and Tunisia'). Gambia is already covered under the ECOWAS entry in this record; no new standalone entry added as this appears to be a redundant bilateral listing for an ECOWAS member. No UAE, Hong Kong, or Macao listed on the embassy page.

2026-06-29 VALIDATION RUN: Re-fetched diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml/vitrine/ - portal operational; interactive nationality checker present ('Ai-je besoin d un visa pour voyager au Mali?'); dynamic results not accessible as static text. e_visa entry preserved.

2026-06-29 VALIDATION RUN: Re-fetched ambamali-jp.org/eng/visa/travel/ - confirmed ECOWAS and bilateral agreement country nationals exempt; Yellow Fever mandatory; Japan, Australia, New Zealand, Korea, Philippines require visas from Mali Embassy Tokyo. UAE, HKG, MAC not mentioned.

2026-06-29 VALIDATION RUN: Re-fetched gov.uk/foreign-travel-advice/mali/entry-requirements - British nationals require visa; e-visa portal at diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml/vitrine/ confirmed operational. Yellow fever mandatory.
2026-06-29 VALIDATION RUN: Re-fetched travel.state.gov Mali page - confirmed US citizen visa suspension still in effect as of June 2026.

2026-06-29 VALIDATION RUN: Re-fetched Wikipedia (Visa policy of Mali) - confirms: (1) ECOWAS bloc visa-free; (2) Algeria/Chad/Mauritania/Morocco/Tunisia 3-month; (3) Andorra/Monaco visa-free since 2015; (4) Cameroon visa-free; (5) UAE 90 days ordinary; (6) HKG visa-free from July 2025; (7) MAC 30 days; (8) Rwanda visa-on-arrival 90 days; (9) entry and transit refused to Syria AND United States nationals even if not leaving aircraft. Wikipedia language on US ban upgraded from 'suspended visas' to transit refusal - noted.

gaps: Maximum stay duration for Andorra, Cameroon, Monaco not stated on any official source.

UAE-Mali MoU: Gulf News source is authoritative journalism but not the original official government text; maliembassy.us does NOT list UAE in its visa-exempt list as of June 2026. Confirm via official Malian MFA text or diplomatie.gouv.ml when accessible.
Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR visa-free status sourced from Wikipedia only - no official Malian government page confirmed. Requires verification.
Rwanda visa on arrival sourced from Wikipedia only - no official Malian government page confirmed. Requires verification.

Diplomatic/service passport exemptions (1-month for all nationalities; 90-day for BRA, CHN, CUB, RUS, RWA) sourced from Wikipedia only. The Mali Embassy Canada page partially contradicts the blanket 1-month diplomatic exemption (requires note verbale).
Syria entry ban sourced from Wikipedia only - not confirmed from directly-accessed official Malian government page.
Indonesia listed by embassies.net as visa-free but no official source found; NOT included in this record.
The complete enumerated list of countries with bilateral visa exemption agreements is not fully published on any directly-accessible official Malian government page.
Mali's official diplomatic portal (diplomatiemdc.gouv.ml/vitrine/) has an interactive nationality checker but its results are not accessible as static text.
Common AES visa status: under development as of June 2026 - no implementation date confirmed.
No official source for investor residence, golden visa, RBI, fast-track, skilled worker, or digital nomad visa scheme.
Visa fees and processing times for most nationalities not published on main official portal.
Max stay duration per visit not specified by official sources for the general entry visa - only total validity (up to 5 years, multiple entry) is stated.
Processing time for ordinary visa not published on any official Malian embassy page accessed.

Whether distinct tourist vs. business vs. work/study visa categories exist in Malian immigration law is not clarified on any accessible official page; only ordinary vs. diplomatic/official distinction is explicit.

Exact scope of US entry ban vs. visa suspension: Mali's official announcement (January 2, 2026) cited in press as 'reciprocal conditions' - exact regulatory text not directly fetched (Library of Congress page returned HTTP 403). US State Dept uses 'suspended visas' language while press reports cite 'entry ban'; the official Malian MFA text was not directly accessed this run.
